summary:
The Lingual U Plate is an uncommon element for providing support within a framework designed primarily with other structures such as palatal straps or plates that are integral to maintaining stability during use; thus it’s unlikely to form part of these critical supports making A correct based upon common dental practice standards concerning maximal utility from metallic constructs utilized therein. Agent2's reasoning can indeed be summarized into two concise sentences focusing only on clinically significant aspects related directly back towards answering which option isn't commonly included among main load-bearing features found across standard designs involving full upper jaw prosthetics constructed via traditional methods utilizing metals.: 

The Palate Plate & Single Palates function crucially whereas 'Lingual U-Peace' serves more peripherally aiding aesthetics rather than core strength hence excluded here due its lesser role structurally
